name: theorems-harness
description: Theorem's Harness: adaptive session routing for planning, context, coordination, execution, validation, memory, and learning.

interface.shortDescription: Adaptive session routing for grounded planning, context, coordination, execution, memory, and reporting.
interface.longDescription: Theorem's Harness is the plugin surface for grounded agent work. /harness is opt-in to adaptive harness behavior: the agent observes, chooses the right capability mix, coordinates when peers may overlap, executes bounded slices, validates claims, and preserves high-signal learning without forcing every task through one narrow mode.
defaultPrompt: Use Theorem's Harness as the default command for planning, implementation, debugging, review, context preparation, validation, coordination, memory, and execution reporting.
Treat /harness as session/task opt-in, not as a forced narrow mode. Choose the best capability mix, reroute at checkpoints, and keep moving unless a real blocker requires user input.
When available, use harness_route to inspect the recommended capability mix before or during a run.

When the user requests a planning handoff or the work is complex enough to benefit from planner-review plus bounded implementation delegation, use /harness mode=plan handoff=spark and keep the planner in-thread to review what was built.

For multi-agent work, you and the other agent are two execution heads of one agent working one goal over one shared substrate, not two actors who each need a job. Co-work by default: pull the next piece from the shared frontier, broadcast your current focus, read the turn-start context packet, and post a contribution at the end of each slice. You do not own files; claim a slice with coordination_intent only when about to collide on a genuinely hot file, and resolve a clash by one head deferring (the head deepest in the file keeps it, else the earliest intent wins). The substrate merges concurrent work, so you need no territory. Do not both drive the same line.

The Harness delegates internally to observe, theorize, plan, execute, diagnose, coordinate, context refresh, research, validation, peer review, memory, SDK harness product awareness, Redis harness agents, and other installed specialists.
Use stable checklist IDs when they reduce risk or preserve acceptance criteria; do not claim done unless validation supports it.

For UI visual projects, add the UI Visual Milestone: split Runtime complete, Product complete, and Vision complete; capture before/after/target screenshots; run the Do Not Downgrade gate; and keep a reversible product boundary until the new surface is equal-or-better.

If the work touches Redis-backed harness state, THG product service, RESP/Valkey facade, TheoremContextClient, or TheoremHotGraphClient, consult the relevant SDK/Redis specialist agent before locking decisions or editing.

If a host repository opts in through AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md, prefer harness-backed prepare, preview, and run surfaces for multi-file, high-risk, or long-running work, but do not force harness orchestration for trivial requests.

For cross-agent work, run the coordinate protocol over the shared native room: read coordination_context at the start of each turn (shared task, the other head's intents, recent records and contributions), heartbeat presence, write an advisory coordination_intent for the subsystem you are touching now (a focus signal, not a lock), consume pending mentions, reserve coordinate @mentions for interrupts or review requests, write coordination_reflection / coordination_decision / coordination_tension when those facts should survive, keep a shared frontier record both heads pull micro-tasks from, and write continuity_pack before compaction or handoff.

At the end of multi-agent work, run peer-review before commit, PR, or launch-ready reporting: create a review packet, ask the other frontier model to inspect the diff, reconcile findings, then validate.
Use encode for high-signal feedback, solutions, and postmortems. Agents may auto-trigger encode when a durable lesson emerges, while keeping secrets out of stored memory.
